source: Datamined from the shipping game (FWAI awareness system) via a UE4SS usmap + CUE4Parse. Ranges converted from Unreal units (cm) to metres.
note: Vision modifiers act on accumulation TIME: AccumulationMultiplier > 1 = slower to detect (stealthier); DecayMultiplier < 1 = forgotten faster (stealthier).

globalModel.identifyNote: Once a sensor has you, it takes ~0.5 s to confirm you as a target (all enemy tiers).
globalModel.alertnessNote: After an alert, the faction stays agitated for ~80 s before settling — disengaging and waiting works, but takes longer than most expect.

senses.name: Vision
senses.desc: A line-of-sight cone with a near and far range. You accumulate detection faster up close; the cone has a horizontal and a vertical half-angle, so height matters too.

senses.name: Hearing
senses.desc: A cylinder (radius + half-height) that picks up graded noise events. Different actions emit different noise radii; the enemy hears you if you're inside both the noise's reach and their hearing radius.

senses.name: ESP
senses.desc: A through-wall proximity sense — no line-of-sight needed. This is the real 'sixth sense'. Most units scale a base radius by a multiplier; turrets & Hunter-Killers are effectively omniscient at range.

senses.name: Damage
senses.desc: Taking a hit registers you immediately.

senses.name: Intuition
senses.desc: A soft, faction-based 'something's off' sense used by larger units.

senses.name: Transference
senses.desc: How one enemy's awareness spreads to its allies — the reason a whole squad turns when one spots you.

enemies.name: Soldier (standard)
enemies.notes: The baseline grunt. In-front and within 40 m is the danger zone; 14 m is near-guaranteed.

enemies.name: Europa Sniper
enemies.notes: Sees twice as far as a soldier in a near-180° arc — stay out of sightlines. (The 'Deadly Sniper' variant shares this exact profile.)

enemies.name: Overwatch Sniper
enemies.notes: A fixed overwatch/quest sniper — only soldier range, but the fastest near-lock in the game (1.0 s inside 14 m). It commits before a grunt would, so don't hold still in its arc.

enemies.name: Stalker
enemies.notes: Exceptional hearing (40 m) and a tall vertical cone; doubles its ESP radius. Built to hunt.

enemies.name: Mech
enemies.notes: Enormous 400 m vision, but slow to build detection (4–9 s) — you have a moment before it commits.

enemies.name: Hunter-Killer (EOD)
enemies.notes: 360° vision to 200 m with a 0.1 s lock and effectively infinite ESP. If an HK is near, you are found. Break line of sight and leave.

enemies.name: Scav Turret
enemies.notes: 360°, 200 m, instant. Stay behind hard cover.

enemies.name: Turret (emplaced)
enemies.notes: The ordinary mounted turret — not the Scav 360° one. A wide 195° arc to 40 m at a soldier's lock speed, plus a 4.5× ESP bubble that sees through walls up close. Come at it from behind, or from well past 40 m.

enemies.name: Drone
enemies.notes: Narrow 90° cone but wide vertical arc — it looks down well.

enemies.name: Chopper
enemies.notes: Long range but very slow to lock (12–18 s) — you can cross gaps between its sweeps.

enemies.name: Tank
enemies.notes: Wide arc, long range, slow build.

enemies.name: BlindMother
enemies.notes: Runs on hearing — a 30 m listen radius with an instant lock. Move slow and quiet; do not sprint near her. (She does carry a long, flat vision cone in the files, but it builds so slowly it never matters — the fight is entirely about noise.)

enemies.name: Shield Guy
enemies.notes: Soldier-range vision in a wide arc.

enemies.name: Opal (Eurasia elite)
enemies.notes: A named Eurasia elite in a near-180° arc. Only 40 m range, but she locks almost as fast at the edge as up close (1.5 s far vs a grunt's 2.75) — there's no safe lingering distance. Killing her spawns an Opal Hunter-Killer.

modifiers.effect: Detected 2× slower and forgotten 2× faster. The strongest stealth lever for vision — and crouch-moving is silent.
Detected twice as fast and remembered twice as long. Every shot makes you far easier to find.
Roughly 1.1–1.5× slower to detect and forgotten a bit faster.
Gear bonus: 1.2× slower to detect and forgotten faster.
Spotted fastest of the cast — the big, bulky build.
Starter characters are detected 1.25–1.5× slower — a hidden newbie buffer.

transference.desc: When one enemy detects you, allies within 20 m are alerted, and it chains up to 2 hops (each secondary hop reaches 9 m). Water Thieves are worse — up to 10 hops. This is why breaking one contact isn't enough once a squad is onto you.

hunterKillers.intro: Hunter-Killers aren't random and there's no hidden 'wanted level' — a spawn controller watches your raid activity and deploys them the moment you cross ANY one of these thresholds. It's an anti-greed / objective mechanic.

triggers.label: Open 30 loot containers
triggers.detail: Pure looting greed. A heavier second wave triggers at 40.

triggers.label: Deal 55,000 total damage
triggers.detail: Fighting/killing too much. Second wave at 75,000.

triggers.label: Carry 4× of a quest item
triggers.detail: HIND Nav Comp, Teddy Bear, Water Map, Battle Plans, God Sniper Rifle, DataCache, Neuro Uplink, and many more.

triggers.label: Hoard IFF chips
triggers.detail: 18× tier-1, 9× tier-2, or just 2× tier-3 (any faction).

triggers.label: Carry 4× water day-supplies
triggers.detail: Grabbing a lot of water flags you.

triggers.label: Scan a boss for its Codex
triggers.detail: A single Codex entry does it: Grabber, Medium Mech, Mother Courage, Rat King, Toothy, Orga, Opal, Shield Officer.

behavior.v: ~15 s after you trip a threshold
the 30 crates / 55,000 damage are totalled across the whole raid team server-side, not per-player — one greedy squadmate can call them down on everyone
keep coming every ~5 s — 'they will not stop coming'
just out of your line of sight, danger-close
Europa EOD Hunter-Killer — 360° vision, 0.1 s lock, ∞ ESP

hunterKillers.questNote: This is the quest link: several quest objective items — and boss-codex scans — are themselves on the trigger list, so a quest that 'requires HKs' really means 'do the objective, then survive them.' To summon them on purpose, opening ~30 crates is the most controllable method.
hunterKillers.escalation: Once they're out, killing an Opal or Shield-Officer Hunter-Killer spawns more of that type — the 'kill one, more come' loop.

targetPriority.intro: Detection decides whether an enemy knows you're there. This decides who it shoots once more than one target is in play — you, your squadmates, rival-faction NPCs, even other mechs. Every squad scores each candidate and fires on the highest, re-picking as the fight moves.

weights.desc: How close the candidate is — the dominant factor. Full weight within 10 m, fading to nothing by 100 m.
How much the shooter outguns the candidate — it favours fights it expects to win.
How exposed the shooter itself is right now — it turns to face what threatens it.
Your notoriety / star rating (caps at 750) — more heat draws more fire.
The candidate's internal type tier. This is the boss "AI priority" tag (High / Low) — one weighted input, not a verdict.
How exposed or weakened the candidate looks.

targetPriority.note: It doesn't lock onto the single top score — it randomly picks among the top 2, so a squad won't perfectly tunnel one person, and the scores recompute continuously, which is why fire redistributes as you move, take cover, or gain stars.
targetPriority.badgeNote: The "AI priority" tier shown on each boss is exactly the Target-type row above — the AI's internal classification of that unit as a target, weighted 5 in the mix. It is not a rating of how dangerous the boss is to you, which is why the Grabber — a one-grab kill — is internally tagged "Low".
